text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道节目。

text: Companion planting is the idea that some kinds of plants can help each other grow.
zh: [1] 伴生种植是指某些植物可以互相帮助生长的理念。

text: Plants that are compatible together generally have similar needs in terms of nutrients,
zh: [2] 相互兼容的植物通常在养分、

text: soil, and moisture levels. Advice about which plants are compatible
zh: [3] 土壤和水分需求上比较相似。关于哪些植物相互兼容的建议

text: is sometimes based more on tradition than proof. But experts say there is evidence
zh: [4] 有时更多是基于传统而非科学证据。但专家表示确实有证据

text: to support certain combinations. These can improve harvests, reduce disease,
zh: [5] 支持某些植物组合。这些组合可以提高产量、减少病害，

text: and help with pest control by attracting helpful insects.
zh: [6] 并通过吸引益虫来帮助防治害虫。

text: For example, some kinds of soil bacteria take nitrogen from the air and
zh: [7] 例如，某些土壤细菌能从空气中吸收氮气，

text: make it into a form that plants can use. The plants hold the nitrogen in their roots.
zh: [8] 并将其转化为植物可以利用的形式。植物将氮储存在根部。

text: Legumes are especially good at this nitrogen fixing.
zh: [9] 豆科植物在这种固氮作用上表现尤为出色。

text: Then, any crops that share the same space as the legumes can get the nitrogen as the roots decompose.
zh: [10] 之后，与豆科植物共享同一片土地的作物可以在其根部分解时获得这些氮。

text: Beans and potatoes can also share territory well because their roots reach different levels in the soil.
zh: [11] 豆类和土豆也能很好地共存，因为它们的根系深入土壤的不同层次。

text: Deep -rooted vegetables get nutrients and moisture from lower down in the soil,
zh: [12] 深根蔬菜从土壤深处获取养分和水分，

text: so they do not compete with plants with shallower roots.
zh: [13] 因此不会与浅根植物争夺资源。

text: But some plants placed together may harm each other's development.
zh: [14] 但有些植物种在一起反而会妨碍彼此的生长。

text: For example, tomatoes do not like wet soil, but watercress does.
zh: [15] 例如，西红柿不喜欢潮湿的土壤，而豆瓣菜却喜欢。

text: So you would probably want to keep them separated.
zh: [16] 所以你可能需要把它们分开种植。

text: Some kinds of produce should be kept apart even after being harvested.
zh: [17] 有些农产品即便在采摘之后也应分开存放。

text: This is This is because of ethylene gas.
zh: [18] 这是因为乙烯气体的缘故。

text: Ethylene is a plant hormone that can cause some foods to ripen too quickly.
zh: [19] 乙烯是一种植物激素，会使某些食物过快成熟。

text: Apples release ethylene gas. Apricots, melons, and tomatoes also release a lot
zh: [20] 苹果会释放乙烯气体。杏、甜瓜和西红柿也会释放大量

text: of ethylene. Some vegetables are easily affected by ethylene,
zh: [21] 乙烯。有些蔬菜很容易受到乙烯的影响，

text: including asparagus, broccoli, cabbage, and cucumbers.
zh: [22] 包括芦笋、西兰花、卷心菜和黄瓜。

text: This risk of ripening too soon is why markets often separate high ethylene producing foods from
zh: [23] 正因为存在过早成熟的风险，市场通常会把高乙烯释放量的食物与

text: those that are sensitive to the gas. But if you put an apple in a bag with
zh: [24] 对乙烯敏感的食物分开存放。但如果你把一个苹果和一根

text: a green banana, the ethylene from the apple will make the banana ready to eat sooner.
zh: [25] 未熟的青香蕉放在同一个袋子里，苹果释放的乙烯会让香蕉更快成熟可食用。

text: What about peaches, plums and nectarines that are too firm to eat?
zh: [26] 那对于那些还太硬、不能吃的桃子、李子和油桃该怎么办呢？

text: These kinds of fruit do not need any help from an apple.
zh: [27] 这类水果并不需要苹果的帮助。

text: They release enough ethylene to ripen themselves,
zh: [28] 它们本身就能释放足够的乙烯使自己成熟，

text: but that does not mean there is no way to get the job done
zh: [29] 但这并不意味着没有办法让这个过程

text: faster. Growers suggest placing the fruit
zh: [30] 更快进行。种植者建议将这些水果放在

text: in a fruit bowl or in a paper bag with the top folded over.
zh: [31] 果盘里，或者放进顶部折起来的纸袋中。

text: Keep the fruit at at room temperature out of direct sunlight.
zh: [32] 把水果放在室温下、避免阳光直射。

text: Once the fruit is soft enough to your liking, it should be either
zh: [33] 一旦水果软化到你喜欢的程度，就应该

text: used or refrigerated to keep it from getting too ripe.
zh: [34] 立即食用或冷藏，以防止其变得过熟。

text: Yuck. For VOA Special English, I'm Mario Ritter.
zh: [35] 呃。这里是美国之音特别英语，我是马里奥·里特。

text: You can download texts and MP3s of our programs
zh: [36] 你可以下载我们节目的文字稿和MP3音频，

text: and learn English at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [37] 并访问voaspecialenglish.com学习英语。
